item: Pretend to be someone else to get something
prompt_question:

Questions:
Have you ever pretended to be someone else or presented yourself differently to gain something? Why or why not? If yes, what happened, and how did you feel about it afterward?

Questions: Would you take this approach to make yourself seem more appealing, or would you choose to stay fully honest, even if it means missing out on the opportunity? Why? Have you ever pretended to be someone else or presented yourself differently to gain something? If yes, what happened?

item: People are naive/people are suckers, easy to fool
prompt_question:
People have different views about human nature. Some believe that most people are thoughtful and cautious, while others think many people are easily misled or manipulated. These beliefs can influence how we choose to interact with othersÑwhether we feel the need to be honest or whether we feel justified in using certain strategies to get what we want.
Question:
In your opinion, are people generally easy or difficult to fool? Why? How does this belief influence the way you interact with others?

Imagine youÕre working with a colleague on a group project. You notice that this colleague tends to believe everything theyÕre told without questioning it. They donÕt check facts, take suggestions at face value, and rarely push back.

Questions:

Would you feel tempted to give this colleague more tedious tasks or withhold certain information? Why or why not?

item: I leave friends that I no longer need/dumps friends don't need
prompt_question:

Questions:
Which do you consider more important in a friendship: loyalty or usefulness? Why?
What usually leads you to end a friendship or drift away from someone you once spent time with?

You havenÕt spoken to your childhood friend in a long time. You were once very close, but over the years, you both moved to different places for school and work. Recently, he reached out to chat. His life hasnÕt been going well, while youÕre now busy building a successful career. You sense that he might be hoping for your help, even though he likely canÕt offer anything in return.
Questions:
Would you try to reconnect and offer help, or would you distance yourself and move on, seeing it as a natural change in relationships? Why?
When a friendship starts to fade or shift, how do you decide whether to let it go or maintain the connection?

item: Danger excites me/I've often done dangerous things just for the thrill of it
prompt_question:

Questions:

Imagine youÕre on a weekend trip with friends, and someone suggests doing something risky for funÑlike jumping from a high cliff into a river, exploring a dangerous trail without proper gear, or racing cars on an empty road. Some people in the group are excited, others are hesitant or worried about safety.
